description_long: This object is a rectangular induction cooker with a flat, smooth surface and four circular burners arranged in a 2x2 grid. Each burner is equipped with a metallic cross-shaped support, likely for stabilizing cookware. The control panel features four round knobs, aligned in a single row along one edge of the cooktop, allowing for individual adjustment of each burner. The surface appears to be made of a hard, heat-resistant material, possibly glass-ceramic or metal, with a matte or slightly smooth finish. There are no visible digital displays, indicator lights, or brand markings on the surface. The overall design is minimalistic and functional, suitable for installation in modern urban kitchens or outdoor cooking setups.
description: A rectangular induction cooker with four burners and four control knobs, featuring a smooth, hard surface and a minimalist design.
description_view_0: Front view showing four burners and the control knobs positioned at the back edge.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the four burners and the row of knobs on the right side.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear look at the four control knobs aligned at the front edge and all four burners.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view showing the burners and the knobs now on the left side.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel consists of four round knobs arranged in a single straight row along one edge of the cooktop, with no visible labels, icons, or indicator lights.
